item.title: Meet the fixer Carney called to save Canada-U.S. trade talks
item.description: The post Meet the fixer Carney called to save Canada-U.S. trade talks appeared first on The Logic .

item.title: Northwestern Ontario pleads for federal funding as highway fatalities mount
item.description: Northern Ontario politicians are urgently appealing to the federal government for help during one of the deadliest winters on record along 2,000 kilometres of highway between Nipigon, Sudbury and North Bay.

item.title: Flurries, freezing rain in store for Toronto before spring officially arrives Friday
Ford government expanding outdoor booze permits on April 30
U.S. hasn’t formally asked NATO to help keep Strait of Hormuz open, Anita Anand says
item.description: Foreign Affairs Minister Anita Anand says the Strait of Hormuz should be demilitarized and open to shipping during the war in Iran, but that t…

item.title: Toronto woman convicted of making antisemitic threat against Crown attorney in courthouse elevator — ‘I froze. I was scared,’ lawyer testifies
Temporary bleachers are being built at BMO Field for the FIFA World Cup. Are they safe?
B.C. premier signals he won't support feds' temporary foreign worker program expansion
item.description: B.C. Premier David Eby has signalled he won't support the federal government's move to temporarily increase rural employers' allowances for temporary foreign workers, saying there should be a pathway to permanent residency instead.

item.title: Longest ballot organizers eye protest in upcoming Montreal-area byelection
item.description: A group that has signed up droves of protest candidates in a number of elections and byelections in recent years is organizing in another upcoming vote, this time in Terrebonne.

item.title: Avi Lewis stands by past activism as he's criticized for 'politics of subtraction'
item.description: In the closing days of the NDP leadership race, Avi Lewis's past activism has resurfaced. But the front-runner's campaign is standing by his past work and a decade-old vision that's been both criticized within the New Democrats' ranks and weaponized by their opponents.

item.title: Politics Insider: Anand says NATO hasn’t been asked to help in the Middle East
item.description: And, New Brunswick’s government tables a $15.6-billion budget driven deep into deficit by health spending

item.title: Federal government appeals Emergencies Act use during convoy protest to Supreme Court
item.description: The federal government is bringing its case to justify use of the Emergencies Act to clear the convoy protests that gridlocked the capital city and border points to the country's highest court.

item.title: Vegan chocolate ice cream recalled in Ontario over undeclared milk
Privacy commissioner says WADA agrees to limit use of athletes’ personal information
item.description: Agreement stems from 2024 complaint WADA shared info to international sporting federations to assess athletes’ sex-based eligibility

item.title: Mark Carney joins call for Israel not to invade Lebanon
Anand says Canada’s focus in Iran war is on unblocking Strait of Hormuz
Toronto police officer injured in stolen vehicle investigation
Ford still 'keen' on Niagara amalgamations, plans to review governance in the region
Minister says CAF ‘not engaged’ in Iran war as questions are asked about support role
Longest ballot protest group returns, targets byelection in Terrebonne
Ontario may allow more drivers to use HOV lanes
item.description: The Ford government is proposing changes to the Highway Traffic Act that would allow single-occupant vehicles to use High Occupancy Vehicle (HOV) lanes during off-peak hours.

item.title: Doug Ford and Mark Carney eye expansion of tax break on new homes
item.description: It would be an expansion of the existing program that exempts first-time homebuyers from the entire tax on new residences that cost less than …

item.title: Doug Ford defends FOI changes, warns of enemies seeking government secrets
French foreign minister suggests Canada could ‘maybe ... at some point’ join EU
A Toronto councillor wants to create public grocery stores to tackle rising food costs. Would that work here?
New Brunswick tables budget with historic $1.4-billion deficit, rise in health spending
item.description: Government plans to cut spending in other areas, notably by shrinking civic service by 12% over three years

item.title: Ontario urges federal government to legalize pepper spray for self-defence
item.description: Ontario is calling on the federal government to legally allow people to carry and use pepper spray for self-defence.

item.title: Electricity demand, natural gas production and renewable power expected to soar by 2050
item.description: Electricity demand is set to boom in Canada by 2050, according to new modelling from the national energy regulator released on Tuesday.

item.title: Ottawa’s Middle East response aims to help Canadians and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, ministers say
item.description: But Defence Minister David McGuinty said Canada does not intend to join the offensive against Iran

item.title: NATO hasn’t received formal request for Strait of Hormuz help, Anand says
item.description: On Monday, U.S. President Donald Trump said 'numerous countries' had told him they were 'on the way' to help the U.S. with the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Thousands attend pro-Palestinian rally in Toronto after judge denies Ford government’s injunction to stop it
item.description: Al-Quds Day is an annual event to show support for Palestine. Critics have called it antisemitic.

item.title: Israel seeking ‘significant change’ to how Canada tackles antisemitism
Canada is copying Europe’s TikTok curbs, but without the teeth
item.description: The post Canada is copying Europe’s TikTok curbs, but without the teeth appeared first on The Logic .

item.title: NDP leadership hopeful rebuffed opponent’s offer of second-ballot support
item.description: A bid by NDP leadership hopeful Heather McPherson to strike a deal with rival Rob Ashton in an effort to stop perceived front-runner Avi Lewis…

item.title: Doug Ford wants to livestream bail hearings. Experts say it would jeopardize the court process: ‘I don’t think that it serves justice’
Why allies aren't leaping to Trump's aid in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump is struggling to persuade other countries to help protect commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz — a development analysts say is partly the result of how he has treated allies since returning to the White House last year.

item.title: Lawful access bill could create vulnerabilities for hackers, experts warn
item.description: Foreign intelligence services with malevolent intent could also exploit intercept infrastructure, tech and legal specialists say

item.title: Longest Ballot Committee collecting signatures for potential candidates in Terrebonne by-election
item.description: Group aims to load up ballots with dozens of little-known candidates as a way to protest electoral system

item.title: Politics Insider: Carney in London for high-profile meetings
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister met with U.K.’s Starmer, discussed war in Iran

item.title: Carney meets Keir Starmer in London as war in Iran disrupts shipping routes
Ford government says public safety concerns behind decision to drop funding for supervised consumption sites
item.description: The province said it will stop funding two sites in Toronto, two in Ottawa, as well as individual sites in Niagara, Peterborough and London, e…

item.title: Premier Ford urges harsh handling of Project South accused charged with conspiring to kill a jail official
Canada can ‘manage’ Trump’s call for help without being drawn into Iran war: minister
Doug Ford says he wants to livestream bail hearings as he announces new jail
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford said Monday his government is exploring the idea of live-streaming bail hearings to the public.

item.title: ‘We got to protect ourselves:’ Doug Ford cites threat from China in defending cabinet secrecy
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford is defending his government’s decision to exclude his office and those of his cabinet ministers from Freedom of Information (FOI) laws, saying the move is necessary to protect the privacy of his constituents and to safeguard information from foreign entities.

item.title: Doug Ford says his cellphone records must remain hidden to protect privacy
item.description: Last week his government announced that the Premier, cabinet ministers and their offices would no longer be subject to freedom of information requests

item.title: Ford accused of limiting transparency law because of cellphone defeat in court
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford saw his attempts to stop his cellphone records being partially released blocked by a panel of judges. Critics say he's changing the law as a result.

item.title: Nature groups warn federal funding cuts endangering efforts to protect land, water
Ottawa puts $200M into space launch pad in Nova Scotia
item.description: Ottawa will use the funds over the next decade to lease a space launch pad near Canso, Nova Scotia, which will serve as the foundation for a future spaceport.

item.title: Carney government in talks with Saskatchewan to fast-track energy projects
Doug Ford wants jets at Billy Bishop. Here’s what is at stake for Toronto’s waterfront — and why some say there’s a ‘missing ingredient’
item.description: The waterfront's value is immeasurable to this city. That means the stakes of an airport expansion and of adding jets to the mix are sky high.

item.title: Doug Ford said ‘whisper jets’ were coming to Billy Bishop. Here’s what aviation experts say about airplane noise
Canada's annual inflation rate fell to 1.8% in February, war's impact not yet reflected
item.description: Canada's annual inflation rate fell to 1.8 per cent in February, Statistics Canada said on Monday.

item.title: Carney, Starmer meet amid deepening Middle East crisis
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney and U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer met in London to discuss the escalating Middle East war and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. The leaders condemned Iran's attacks, warned of mounting civilian and economic costs, and stressed the need to reopen the critical oil shipping route.

item.title: Canada joins allies in warning against Israeli ground offensive in Lebanon
item.description: "The humanitarian situation ⁠in Lebanon, including ongoing mass displacement, is already deeply alarming," said a statement by ​Canada, France, Britain, Germany and Italy.

item.title: Poilievre arrives late to the auto debate with a plan from 1965
item.description: It’s as if the Conservative Leader can’t believe the happy days are gone and thinks a simple plan will bring them back

item.title: Poilievre pitches tariff-free auto industry pact with U.S.
item.description: Conservative Leader says Canada can’t steer away from its biggest export market if it wants to revive sector

item.title: Company offers use of private deep-water Baffin Island port to Department of National Defence
item.description: Defence Minister was non-committal when asked about the possibility of the military using Milne Port

item.title: Doug Ford to seek an injunction to block Al-Quds Day rally in Toronto
Canadian competitive sports shooters caught in the crosshairs of sweeping federal gun control reforms
Poilievre unveils auto plan aiming for tariff-free access to U.S. market
item.description: Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre unveiled the party's most substantial auto strategy to date under his leadership — one he says will both bring back production to Canada and be "highly attractive" to partners south of the border.

item.title: Canada, Nordic leaders aim to deepen ties as trade being used as ‘coercive tool’
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney is concluding his time in Norway meeting with that country’s prime minister, along with the leaders of Denmark, Iceland, Sweden and Finland.
